Joinder In 661 Howard Streets Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Mar-29-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Frank Cialone, Esq., Shartsis Friese LLP, 415-421-6500, appearing for Plaintiff, Harold Brandell (courtCall);
Michael Krieger, Esq., Newmeyer & Dillion LP, 925-988-3200, apeparing for Plaintiff Thristy Bear; (CourtCall)
Peter Bales, Esq., Buchalter, 415-227-0900, appearing for Defendant 661 HOWARD STREET LLC;

RULING - A R G U E D. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT THIRSTY BEAR BREWING COMPANY LLC'S Joinder In 661 Howard Street's Motion To Compel Arbitration.

Defendant (and landlord) 661 Howard Street LLC's motion to compel arbitration is granted. The subject lease's arbitration language - "[a]ny controversy or claim arising out of or relating to this" lease - is quite broad, and California's policy favoring arbitration is quite strong. (Khalatian v. Prime Time Shuttle, Inc. (2015) 237 Cal.App.4th 651, 659-60; Moncharsh v. Heily & Blase (1992) 3 Cal.4th 1, 9.) Plaintiffs' argument that their complaint is not related to the lease is belied by the complaint's first paragraph; it repeats the word "lease" six times, pleading, e.g., that "lease payments'substantially exceed the Landlord's direct costs of ownership."

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600 email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)

MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION
Off Calendar Mar-29-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Frank Cialone, Esq., Shartsis Friese LLP, 415-421-6500, appearing for Plaintiff, Harold Brandell
Michael Krieger, Esq., Newmeyer & Dillion LP, 925-988-3200, appearing for Plaintiff Thirsty Bear; (CourtCall)
Peter Bales, Esq., Buchalter, 415-227-0900, appearing for Defendant 661 HOWARD STREET LLC;

RULING - A R G U E D. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT 661 HOWARD STREET LLC'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION.

Defendant (and landlord) 661 Howard Street LLC's motion to compel arbitration is granted. The subject lease's arbitration language - "[a]ny controversy or claim arising out of or relating to this" lease - is quite broad, and California's policy favoring arbitration is quite strong. (Khalatian v. Prime Time Shuttle, Inc. (2015) 237 Cal.App.4th 651, 659-60; Moncharsh v. Heily & Blase (1992) 3 Cal.4th 1, 9.) Plaintiffs' argument that their complaint is not related to the lease is belied by the complaint's first paragraph; it repeats the word "lease" six times, pleading, e.g., that "lease payments'substantially exceed the Landlord's direct costs of ownership."

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600 email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)
